author: Santokh Singh, B.
title: Jind te sarir da charcha ate kaul karar
gloss: Admonitory verses in the form of a dialogue between the soul and the body.

title: Sardar Bagh Singh, Faujdar
gloss: The story of a Sikh soldier in verse showing how brave and true to their duty and religion the Sikh soldiers are.
notes: Martial-loyalty ideal of the Sikh soldier in tract form.

author: Sawan Singh, B.
title: Temperance Git, Hissa chautha, arthat Siapa Sharab
publisher: The author, who is a Sub-Divisional Officer at Abbott-abad
notes: Temperance series part 4 ('The Mourning of Liquor'); colonial-service author. Date printed '2th September'.

author: Sham Das, P.
title: Prichha
gloss: Select verses from the Sikh Grantha.

author: Sham Das, P.
title: Jangnama Guru Gobind Singh Ji Patshahi 10
gloss: Verses on the battles between Guru Gobind Singh, the tenth Sikh Guru, and Aurangzeb, the Mugal Emperor of Delhi.
notes: Hindu pandit author/publisher pair producing Sikh jangnama.

title: Shangar Lanat
gloss: Verses directed against the evil practice of locking up money in the form of ornaments.
notes: Kairon anti-ornament campaign continues from Q3 (Shangar Judh, Narki Gehna cluster) — now 3,000 copies via a village diwan.

author: Sher Singh, Gyani, B.
title: Siri Amritsar ji di Sugat, Pehla Bhag
gloss: Verses emphasising the importance of female chastity.
publisher: The Khalsa Sat Sang Sabha, Chirk, District Ferozepore

title: Sugandhit asthan arthat Farsi de prasidh Kavi Shaikh Sadi Krit 'Bostan' da ultha, Chewan kand
gloss: Translation into Gurmukhi verse of Bostan by Shaikh Sadi, Chapter 6th.
publisher: B. Bal Singh, Subedar-Major, President, Khalsa Diwan, Malaya
notes: Sa'di's Bostan (ch. 6) in Gurmukhi verse, sponsored from Malaya by the same diaspora Subedar-Major as p7 s42 — Persian classic → Sikh diaspora → Punjabi print.
